key_points: Elections are essential for choosing representatives and forming governments in a democracy
Democratic elections must provide real choice to voters among different candidates and parties
Political competition encourages leaders to perform better to win public support
Universal Adult Franchise ensures that every citizen above 18 years has equal voting rights
The country is divided into electoral constituencies to ensure equal representation
The voters list or electoral roll contains the names of eligible voters in each constituency
Reserved constituencies ensure representation of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes
Election campaigns allow candidates to present their policies and ideas to voters
Polling is the process where voters cast their votes at polling booths
The Election Commission of India supervises elections to ensure they are free and fair
examples: National election example: Lok Sabha elections
State election example: Legislative Assembly elections
Technology used in voting: Electronic Voting Machine (EVM)
Example of political campaign: Public meetings and rallies
Example of reserved seats: Constituencies reserved for SC/ST candidates
Example of election supervision: Election observers monitoring polling stations
formulas: Democratic Election → Election where people freely choose representatives
Political Competition → Contest among parties and candidates to win elections
Constituency → Geographic area represented by an elected member
Electoral Roll → Official list of eligible voters in a constituency
By-election → Election held to fill a vacant legislative seat
Voter Turnout → Percentage of registered voters who actually vote
exam_tips: Remember that elections must be conducted regularly in a democracy
Universal adult franchise is a key feature of democratic elections
Election symbols help illiterate voters identify parties
Reserved constituencies ensure fair representation of weaker sections
Election Commission ensures fairness and transparency in elections
